skill.name: writing-plans
skill.description: Create detailed implementation plans from specs before code changes begin.
skill.summary: Turns requirements into step-by-step, test-focused implementation plans.
skill.author: obra

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution and weak cryptography patterns, but review found only Markdown formatting, example commands, and planning prose. No executable code, network access, credential handling, prompt injection attempt, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Treated as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The 16 external command alerts point to Markdown inline code, fenced examples, and illustrative pytest or git commands inside a planning template. These are not executable skill code. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Confidence: 0.96. Confidence reasoning: Direct review of each cited SKILL.md location shows prose or Markdown examples, with no Ruby backtick execution or runtime command invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct review of each cited SKILL.md location shows prose or Markdown examples, with no Ruby backtick execution or runtime command invocation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Not Present
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts at the cited lines do not contain cryptographic algorithms, hashing calls, or security-sensitive code. The lines are frontmatter description, audience assumptions, and a plan goal placeholder. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Confidence: 0.94. Confidence reasoning: The cited SKILL.md lines contain plain planning text only, and no crypto API or weak algorithm name is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited SKILL.md lines contain plain planning text only, and no crypto API or weak algorithm name is present.

content.value_statement: Complex engineering work often fails when requirements are not translated into concrete tasks. This skill creates detailed, test-focused plans with files, commands, and handoff steps.
actual_capabilities: Structures implementation plans with goals, architecture notes, and tech stack context.
Breaks work into small developer tasks with exact files to create, modify, or test.
Prompts for failing tests, minimal implementation, passing verification, and commits.
Includes example test commands and expected outcomes inside the plan format.
Adds an execution handoff that offers subagent-driven or parallel-session execution paths.
limitations: Does not inspect code automatically unless the AI tool separately reads the project files.
Does not execute tests, commit code, or verify implementation results by itself.
Assumes a development workflow that values TDD and frequent commits.
May need adaptation for non-code projects or teams with different planning formats.

use_cases.title: Plan a Feature Build
use_cases.description: Turn a product spec into ordered implementation tasks with tests, files, and commands.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Handoff Document
use_cases.description: Create a plan that another developer can follow without prior project context.

use_cases.title: Standardize Test-First Work
use_cases.description: Frame a change as failing tests, minimal implementation, verification, and commits.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with a short requirement.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the writing-plans skill to create an implementation plan for this requirement: [paste requirement]. Include tasks, files, tests, and commands.

prompt_templates.title: Plan From a Product Spec
prompt_templates.scenario: Use a more complete product or engineering brief.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the writing-plans skill to convert this spec into a detailed implementation plan: [paste spec]. Include architecture notes and exact task order.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Refactor Safely
prompt_templates.scenario: Apply the planning structure to a risky refactor.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the writing-plans skill to plan this refactor: [describe refactor]. Emphasize regression tests, small commits, and safe rollback points.

prompt_templates.title: Plan With Execution Handoff
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced plan that another session or agent will execute.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the writing-plans skill to write a complete implementation handoff for [feature]. Assume the executor has no context and needs exact files, test commands, and checkpoints.

output_examples.input: Plan a new password reset flow for an existing web app.
output_examples.output: A plan with the goal, architecture notes, affected routes and services, failing tests for reset requests, implementation tasks, verification commands, and commit checkpoints.

output_examples.input: Create a plan to refactor billing validation without changing behavior.
output_examples.output: A task sequence that starts with characterization tests, isolates validation logic, updates callers, runs focused and full test suites, and records review checkpoints.

output_examples.input: Write a handoff plan for adding CSV export to an admin dashboard.
output_examples.output: A developer-ready document covering UI changes, backend export handling, permissions tests, exact file paths, expected command results, and execution options.

best_practices: Provide the source spec, target repository context, and known constraints before asking for a plan.
Review generated file paths and commands against the real project before execution begins.
Keep each task small enough to test, review, and commit independently.
anti_patterns: Using the plan as a substitute for reading the actual codebase.
Combining many unrelated features into one large plan.
Skipping the failing-test and verification steps because the change seems simple.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates detailed implementation plans for multi-step engineering work before code changes start.

faq.question: Does it write or change code?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on planning. Code changes require a separate execution step.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the requirement, relevant project context, constraints, and any files or systems that may be affected.

faq.question: Can it help with test-driven development?
faq.answer: Yes. The plan format emphasizes failing tests, minimal implementation, verification, and commits.

faq.question: Is it only for Claude?
faq.answer: No. The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it be used for refactors?
faq.answer: Yes. It can structure refactors around characterization tests, incremental changes, and verification commands.
